svg.text: BENG207_2 Configuration B: Dual-Transducer Immersed in Water
PZT → water gap → PDMS wall → water channel (organoids) → PDMS wall → water gap → PZT
Config B Advantages (vs. Config A single-source)
• Contrast: ∞ (perfect nodes) — independent of wall material (PDMS |r| = 0.17 doesn't matter)
• Phase control: sweep φ from 0→π shifts nodes by λ/4 = 0.5 mm (fine-tune CI electrode alignment)
• Symmetric: equal trapping force at all nodes (uniform organoid size)
• No couplant gel: PZT immersed in water, no aging/degradation (eliminates BENG207_1 failure mode)
Based on Hou et al., Extreme Mech. Lett. 37, 100716 (2020) — adapted for 1D organoid spacing
